Class PooledConnectionHolder

java.lang.Object
edu.iu.jdbc.pool.PooledConnectionHolder
class PooledConnectionHolder extends Object
Holds a PooledConnection.

    Method
    Description
    (package private) Instant
    getLastUse()
    Gets the last time the connection was used.
    (package private) int
    getUsageCount()
    Gets the number of times the connection has been used.

    usageComplete()
    Increments the number of times the connection has been used, and sets the last usage time to the current time.

    Invoked when a connection is checked out from the pool.

    Validates pooledConnection as connected and usable.

    • pooledConnection

      final PooledConnection pooledConnection
      PooledConnection instance.

    • initiated

      final Instant initiated
      Instant the connection was initiated.

  • Constructor Details

    • PooledConnectionHolder

      PooledConnectionHolder(PooledConnection pooledConnection, Instant initiated)
      Constructor
      Parameters:
      pooledConnection - PooledConnection instance
      initiated - Instant the connection was initiated.

  • Method Details

    • usageStarted

      void usageStarted(ScheduledFuture<?> reaperTask)
      Invoked when a connection is checked out from the pool.
      Parameters:
      reaperTask - abandoned connection reaper task to be canceled by usageComplete()

    • usageComplete

      void usageComplete()
      Increments the number of times the connection has been used, and sets the last usage time to the current time.

    • getLastUse

      Instant getLastUse()
      Gets the last time the connection was used.
      Returns:
      last time the connection was used

    • getUsageCount

      int getUsageCount()
      Gets the number of times the connection has been used.
      Returns:
      number of times the connection has been used

    • validate

      void validate(String validationQuery) throws SQLException
      Validates pooledConnection as connected and usable.
      Parameters:
      validationQuery - SQL to execute on the connection, should produce at least one row with a non-null value in the first column
      Throws:
      SQLException - if the logical connection cannot be established or the validation query fails
